metadata.notes: agent=yes daemon=full driver=ready — UNBLOCKED by subagent linking (antigravity_subagent.go). With the child now modelled as a linked session (parent_linked), the #321 no-working-on-cleanup invariant finally has something to assert. Recipe: spawn parent + 1 foreground subagent, wait for parent_linked, SIGKILL the parent's agy process; the pid_manager sweep reaps the parent (process_exited) and the orphaned child via deleteWithChildren WITHOUT a spurious state_transition to working for the child. The child has no own process (pid=0) — it is reaped through the parent's children cascade. driver sigkill step is implemented.

assessment.body:
## Verdict

**agent=yes.** Antigravity has subagents (`run_subagent`/`define_subagent`/`invoke_subagent`, `GetSubagents`/`KillSubagent`/`SubagentSpec` in v1.0.10), so a parent CAN launch a foreground child that becomes orphaned when the parent's process is SIGKILLed. Capable model required (Gemini 3.1 Pro High / 3.5 Flash).

**daemon=full (UNBLOCKED).** Previously frozen `incapable` precisely because the daemon never modelled the child as a distinct session, so there was no orphan to sweep and no #321 invariant to assert. Subagent linking removes both blockers: the child is now its own session (`brain/<child>/transcript.jsonl` → `transcript_new`) and is bound to the parent via `AntigravityParentConvID` → `parent_linked` (helpers.go:102) — the recipe's precondition (`parent_linked` fires before the SIGKILL) is now satisfiable. On the SIGKILL of the parent's agy process, the `pid_manager` sweep emits `process_exited` for the parent and reaps the orphaned child through `deleteWithChildren` (pid_manager.go), WITHOUT a spurious `state_transition` to `working` for the orphaned child during cleanup — the #321 invariant. The child has no own process (pid=0, runs inside the parent's invoke), so it is reaped via the parent's children cascade rather than its own dead-PID detection — the no-working-transition invariant is identical for any N≥1, so the minimal 1-child shape suffices.

**driver=ready.** The recipe needs `send` + `wait_turn` + `sigkill`. All three are implemented in `driver-interactive.sh` — `step_sigkill` resolves the agy PID and `kill -9`s it, leaving the dead pane for teardown so the kill alone produces process_exited (driver L500 + step_sigkill). No driver gap (unlike gemini-cli, whose scaffold lacked sigkill).
caveats: daemon=full is derived from the linking code + the standard #321 cleanup machinery; the actual process_exited for parent AND the orphaned-child cleanup WITHOUT a working flicker are PROVISIONAL until record confirms (confidence 0.7).
The orphaned child has no own agy process (pid=0) — it is reaped via the parent's deleteWithChildren cascade, not per-child dead-PID detection. The no-working invariant holds either way.
parent_killed / child_cleanup should anchor on a LANDED phase (parent_turn_start) so a provisional parent_linked doesn't cascade — mirrors the gemini-cli 3.4 sibling anchoring.
observations omit cost/tokens — antigravity's JSONL carries no usage.

sources.note: AntigravityParentConvID makes the child a linked session — without it there is no orphan to sweep (the old incapable reason)
SweepDeadPIDs / deleteWithChildren reaps the parent and cascades to the orphaned child on process_exited, without a working transition (#321)
sigkill step implemented (step_sigkill: kill -9 the agy PID) — no driver gap
run_subagent / KillSubagent / SubagentSpec — the subagent feature

preconditions: agy CLI on PATH (1.0.10), Google OAuth authenticated; no API key
A CAPABLE model selected (Gemini 3.1 Pro High or Gemini 3.5 Flash) — GPT-OSS 120B fumbles the invoke_subagent schema
irrlichd in --record mode with grant-all permission mode (transcripts permission)
A small file in the workspace cwd for the child to read (gives the child enough work to still be alive at SIGKILL time)
setup: Driver launches agy under tmux in a fresh workspace cwd; the parent conv-id session binds pid>0 (history.jsonl cwd).
The send spawns ONE foreground subagent doing a slow task; wait_turn is NOT used to wait for completion — instead a short sleep lets parent_linked fire, then sigkill orphans the still-running child.

script.text: Use a subagent to read every file in this directory one at a time and summarize each — take your time and be thorough.

verify: the parent conv-id session: ready → working at spawn
a child session appears (transcript_new) and parent_linked binds child → parent BEFORE the sigkill
after sigkill: process_exited for the parent
the orphaned child is reaped (removed/process_exited via deleteWithChildren) WITHOUT a state_transition to working/waiting during cleanup (#321)
all sessions disappear after the sweep; final state ready
